---
title: "Multi-omics Clinical Validation (TCGA-BRCA)"
---

## Multi-Omic Integration Strategy

We evaluated the OLEE using the TCGA-BRCA dataset ($N=549$ samples). The dataset includes three modalities:
*   **RNA-Seq:** 604 gene features.
*   **Copy Number Variation (CNV):** 860 regional features.
*   **Proteomics (RPPA):** 223 protein markers.

The target variable is Estrogen Receptor (ER) Status (Positive vs. Negative).

## Performance Results

We used 5-fold cross-validation to compare the Linear Accuracy ($Acc_{XV}$) of the SiMLR baseline against the LEND, NED, and NEDPP architectures. All models were evaluated on their linear basis.

| Model | Accuracy (Mean ± SD) |
| :--- | :---: |
| **SiMLR** | 0.898 ± 0.028 |
| **NED** | 0.914 ± 0.014 |
| **NEDPP** | 0.920 ± 0.014 |
| **LEND** | **0.922 ± 0.014** |

Deep alignment improved mean predictive accuracy and reduced variance compared to the linear baseline. This indicates that the non-linear decoder stabilizes the linear subspace used for classification.

## Tri-Modal Feature Discovery

The OLEE enables extraction of weights from the deep model. Figure @fig-brca-trimodal-waterfall shows the top 15 weights discovered by the LEND architecture across three modalities.

![**Tri-Modal Feature Weights for ER-Status.** LEND identifies markers across modalities. (Left) RNA-Seq weights for transcription factors. (Center) CNV regional weights. (Right) Proteomics weights for ER-alpha and GATA3. The alignment prioritizes markers with cross-modal consistency.](figures/fig_brca_trimodal_waterfall.png){#fig-brca-trimodal-waterfall width="100%"}

### Mechanistic Alignment

LEND provides a direct audit trail for feature weights. In the proteomics panel, ER-alpha is the primary driver. This signal aligns with the ESR1 transcript in the RNA-Seq panel. LEND assigns higher weights to protein-level markers such as INPP4B and GATA3 compared to linear methods. 

These markers are consistent with established oncology data. ESR1 expression determines ER-positive classification [@2012]. GATA3 is a pioneer factor co-expressed with ER-alpha in luminal breast cancer [@Cohen_2014; @Prat_2019]. INPP4B loss is associated with PI3K pathway activation and correlates with ER status [@2012; @2016]. The deep alignment mechanism resolves relationships between CNV, RNA, and protein expression to prioritize these features.
